question.text: Under the SEBI (Substantial Acquisition of Shares and Takeovers) Regulations, 2011, an acquirer must make a public announcement of an open offer when acquiring shares entitling voting rights of:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 3(1) of the SAST Regulations states that any acquirer acquiring shares or voting rights entitling 25% or more voting rights must make a public announcement of an open offer. **The Trap:** The 5% threshold under Regulation 3(2) relates to additional acquisition within a financial year for those already holding 25%+, not the initial trigger. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series IX (Merchant Banking) also covers takeover regulations extensively. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** For listed entities on Innovators Growth Platform, the threshold is 49% instead of 25%.
